column1: Overall Ranked Shortlist - 15 most useful sources across all categories
Ranked by usefulness to your paddy-rice IOC model and data quality, balanced across C1-C6. Per-category shortlists remain inside each category tab. UNVERIFIED DOIs need confirmation before citing.

column6: why it is in the top 15
Best system match: paired soil + void(pore)water + brown rice for 17 PFAS across 30 KOREAN paddy fields. The primary calibration anchor.
Foundational sorption QSPR: +0.50-0.60 log/CF2, +0.23 for sulfonate, OC dominance, Ca2+/pH effects. Backbone of the soil sub-model.
K_MW + K_PW for 60 PFAS; per-CF2 slopes (+0.36 PFCA, +0.37 PFSA) and HSA optimum C6-C10. Best single binding source.
Michaelis-Menten active uptake + channel-inhibitor dissection; U-shaped root-BAF. Mechanistic core for the carrier term.
First nationwide paired soil-grain set (n=160, rice/wheat/maize, 11 provinces). Background-level BAF reference.
PFOA pKa ~ -0.5; the key reference justifying the permanently-anionic (f_d=~1) assumption.
Membrane permeability P_d (+ K_MW) for PFAAs & alternatives - direct input to the GHK/diffusive root term.
Clean Koc values (710 / 96 / 17 mL/g), OC correlation, and reversibility/hysteresis behaviour.
PFAA binding to PLANT proteins (soy protein isolate = storage-protein/grain analog) vs animal albumin; QSAR. Most relevant to plant B_k.
Log-linear BAF decrease with chain length and quantified short-chain uptake bias across 13 crops on a contamination gradient.

column2: ORYZA2000 (Bouman et al. 2001; Bouman & van Laar 2006)
column6: The forcing-function GENERATOR: DVS-based biomass partitioning, LAI growth, dry-matter distribution, daily transpiration for M_k(t) and Q_TP(t).
Predictive sorption model for PFCAs & PFSAs vs chain length and functional group - directly parameterizes the soil sub-model.
Identifies root cell-wall PECTIN & HEMICELLULOSE as the PFAS binding sites controlling root-to-shoot translocation (B_1 / K_cw).
RICE root membrane potential (-116 to -140 mV) - direct input to the Nernst/GHK anion-exclusion term. Rare rice-specific E_m.
Rice crop coefficients (1.05 / 1.20 / 0.90-0.60) and Penman-Monteith ET0 to drive the transpiration stream Q_TP(t).
